composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the main backend code (e.g., app.py) for the OnlineLibrary web application.
- The backend must be accessible via local port 5000.
- The first page should be accessible at route '/' (Dashboard page).
- Implement routing for all frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- File read/write operations must start from the first line of the file, using plain text without encryption.
- The backend should handle all business logic including user management, book catalog, borrowings, reservations, reviews, fines, and payments.
- Frontend code files to be supported by backend routes  dashboard.html, book_catalog.html, book_details.html, borrow_confirmation.html, my_borrowings.html, reservations.html, reviews.html, review_edit.html, profile.html, payment_confirmation.html.
- Incorporate the original task’s supplementary condition  the website starts from the Dashboard page.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop frontend pages for user interaction and navigation.
- Implement all frontend HTML templates with proper IDs and elements as specified in the requirements.
- All URLs in the frontend code should be routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names.
- Frontend pages include  dashboard.html, book_catalog.html, book_details.html, borrow_confirmation.html, my_borrowings.html, reservations.html, reviews.html, review_edit.html, profile.html, payment_confirmation.html.
- Ensure UI elements such as buttons, inputs, dropdowns, tables, and div containers match the specified IDs and types.
- Implement navigation and interaction flows between pages using route URLs.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop utility Python modules for data management and business logic.
- utils/data_loader.py  Functions to load and parse text files from the 'data' directory.
- utils/user_manager.py  Manage user data updates and retrieval.
- utils/borrow_manager.py  Manage borrowings, returns, and status updates.
- utils/reservation_manager.py  Manage reservations and their updates.
- utils/review_manager.py  Manage writing, editing, listing, and deleting reviews.
- utils/fine_manager.py (optional but recommended)  Manage fines calculation and payment status.
- Each module should handle file read/write operations starting from the first line, using plain text with pipe-delimited format as specified.
- These modules will be used by the backend code to implement business logic cleanly and modularly.
